role_summary: Reverend; pastor of Mt. Zion Baptist Church in Seattle (1958-1998); civil rights movement leader; in this corpus's embedding his testimony clusters with Amos C. Brown, Robert L. Carter, Charles McLaurin, and the SCLC-and-NAACP cohort.
ai_reading: McKinney's top semantic neighbor is Amos C. Brown (entry 2, count 122), the San Francisco NAACP and Third Baptist pastor whose own career parallels McKinney's Seattle Mt. Zion Baptist pastorate. The pairing of two long-serving West Coast Black Baptist civil rights pastors is the catalog's cleanest same-vocation-and-region cosine outside the BPP-brothers and lawyer cohort pairings. McKinney's other neighbors are Robert L. Carter (entry 108, NAACP LDF), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, Mississippi SNCC), C. T. Vivian (entry 134, SCLC), and Reverend Harry Blake (entry 102). His position on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis is +0.84, far toward Armed Self-Defense. This continues the catalog's recurring AI-vs-culture inversion on this axis (Walker +0.87, Jones +0.67, Lowery +0.78, Worth W. Long +0.72, Long-Eldorado-Self-Defense-and-Seeger-and-now-McKinney all on the side opposite their cultural placements as nonviolent SCLC-network pastors).
biographical_paragraph: Samuel Berry McKinney's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 115 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.

sources.title: Samuel Berry McKinney oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
